title: Two Skills Miss Steps You Keep Doing by Hand

workflow_patterns.title: Planning Every Step Before Starting
workflow_patterns.description: You ask for a website and the agent writes out a full plan before touching any files. It lists every piece it will make and waits for your go-ahead.

workflow_patterns.title: Preview, Feedback, and Fix Rounds
workflow_patterns.description: The agent opens the site, shares a picture, and waits for notes. You flag wrong pictures or a button that will not close. The agent fixes each spot and shares a new picture.

workflow_patterns.title: Finding Pictures That Match the Topic
workflow_patterns.description: The agent looks online for pictures that fit your subject and drops them into the site so each card shows the right image.

evolutions.description: Adds a picture-picking rule and a before-screenshot check to the example-skill-frontend-design skill.

edits.new_string:
## Picking Pictures That Fit the Subject

Never plug in random placeholder images. For every card, pick a picture that matches the subject (food for a recipe, a place for a travel item). Use a steady source so the same picture shows up every time the page loads, and give each image a short text label so the page still makes sense if the picture is missing.

## Before-Screenshot Check

Before sharing a screenshot with the user, click through the site yourself:

- every button and close-mark does something
- pop-ups close by the close-mark, by clicking outside, and by pressing escape
- the search box filters the cards
- the page still looks right on a phone-width window

Catching these here saves a feedback round.

evolutions.rationale:
The skill does not stop random pictures from landing on the site.
- A picture rule prevents mismatched images
- A pre-check would catch stuck buttons before review
addressed_patterns: Finding Pictures That Match the Topic

evolutions.description: Adds a click-through script and screen-size screenshots to the example-skill-webapp-testing skill.

edits.new_string:
## Click-Through Before the Screenshot

Before the first screenshot, run a short script that opens each pop-up and closes it three ways: by the close-mark, by clicking outside, and by pressing escape. Flag any case that did not work. This catches buttons that look fine in a picture but do nothing when clicked.

## Screenshots at Three Screen Sizes

Always capture the site at three widths: phone (about 375 pixels), tablet (about 768 pixels), and desktop (about 1440 pixels). Show all three to the user at once so layout problems on small screens cannot hide behind a desktop-only view.

evolutions.rationale:
The skill checks only what a single screenshot shows.
- A click-through catches dead buttons a picture cannot
- Three screen sizes expose layout issues on phones
